articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A toxic high pressure system of caste hatred dominated the board, bringing with it a torrent of violent threats against low castes. This dark front was punctuated by scattered calls for beheadings, acid attacks, and other forms of brutal violence.

A separate low-pressure system of erotic asphyxiation discussions moved in, bringing reports of deaths during livestreams and online activities. This system lingered, with Anon sharing cases from Italy and elsewhere.

Meanwhile, a cold snap of misogyny and incel frustration gripped parts of the board, with one anon inquiring about rapid 'transformation' from incel to 'Chad' in just two months.

Elsewhere, a drizzle of religious slurs fell, with Buddhists being called 'dumb' and 'atheist', and Hindus being praised for their perceived cultural practices.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Violence Against Low Castes, Others Join
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a post calling for violence against low castes, including killing, beheading, and other forms of brutal harm.

The post listed specific violent actions, such as dropping low castes in a vat of acid, using them as manure, and setting them on fire.

Another anon responded by claiming that low castes are responsible for India's poverty and that they have ruined various Indian institutions. This anon described low castes as "dirty, smelly" and implied they are to blame for the country's problems.

A third anon suggested allowing low castes to use upper caste surnames as a way to address social issues. However, this anon also expressed concern that low castes who use upper caste surnames would then commit crimes and blame upper castes for their actions.

One respondent brought up a specific case of a dalit man accused of raping a five-year-old girl, and how the dalit community allegedly abandoned him.

This anon noted that the dalit man was named after Bhimta Rao, a figure associated with the dalit rights movement. Anon also discussed the role of reservations in Indian society, with some arguing that they are a form of unfair advantage for low castes.

One respondent described those who use reservations as "dysgenic persons" and expressed a desire to "destroy" them.

The conversation also touched on the idea of ethnic pride and spiritual duty in Hinduism, with some anons arguing that these concepts are used to justify discrimination against low castes.

Others discussed the idea of "epigenetics" and how it relates to the alleged behavioral traits of low castes.

Throughout the thread, anons made various inflammatory and discriminatory statements about low castes, including claims that they are prone to committing crimes and are "subhuman." Some anons also expressed a desire to harm or kill low castes, and discussed ways to "elevate" themselves and others through intermarriage.

articles.title: Anon shares cases of erotic asphyxiation deaths
articles.body:
Anon posted about a 24-year-old Onlyfans and PornHub erotic gay model who died during a livestream in Italy during the COVID-19 pandemic.

The model was using a high-quality pirated VPN, which delayed the discovery of his location and corpse by at least 24 hours.

Another anon responded with a case from a 2004 German Forensic Medicine textbook, where a 24-year-old man intentionally caused the death of his 28-year-old gay partner during a strangulation fetish session.

The man had been acting as a safe spotter but encouraged his partner to push the session further.

Anon also shared cases of individuals who died during erotic asphyxiation, including a 23-year-old woman who died while her partner was distracted and a 27-year-old man who passed out while streaming on LiveJasmin.

A 25-year-old medical nurse and online model from China also died during erotic asphyxiation, with a lubricated vibrator found at her feet.

Some anons discussed the possible reasons behind the arousal from hanging or strangulation, with one suggesting that it may be due to the hallucinogenic effect caused by the brain being deprived of blood.

Others shared their personal experiences, with one stating that they had been watching gore since they were 13 but found hanging videos to be particularly uncomfortable.

The thread also included some who expressed concern and criticism, with one anon stating that the thread was painful to read and another suggesting that those who were uncomfortable with the content should simply watch other threads.

articles.title: Incel to Chad Transformation in 2 Months
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion by asking for advice on transforming from an incel to a 'Chad' in just two months.

One respondent suggested that getting in shape was a feasible goal within that timeframe, but changing one's personality was harder, especially for those with autism.

Another anon recommended a high-carb diet and exercise, specifying at least 200 grams of simple carbohydrates daily.

A different poster suggested stopping habits like excessive masturbation and focusing on physical appearance, such as going to the gym, eating protein, sleeping well, and growing a beard.

Some anons exchanged views on unrelated topics, including types of underwear and Hindi profanity. One individual jokingly asked for tips on memorizing the Fibonacci sequence.

A few respondents engaged in a lighthearted exchange about posting content, including images and sequences. One anon also mentioned the idea of getting a vasectomy due to economic concerns.

The discussion also involved conversations about individual experiences and advice on self-improvement. Anon shared a personal story of being in a relationship and wanting to support their partner as a homemaker.

The conversation remained focused on the original question and tangential advice.

articles.title: Anon sparks thread with 'Gobarida edition' remark
articles.body:
Anon initiated a thread with a two-word post: 'Gobarida edition.' Other anons responded with confusion, asking for clarification on the term. Some speculated it could refer to a book, product, or cultural reference.

The conversation quickly devolved into unrelated topics, including discussions about fictional robot waifus and societal issues.

One anon fantasized about a future with a skewed male-to-female ratio, suggesting '4 FEMALE PER MALE HO JAAYE.' Another anon expressed frustration with the current state of affairs, stating 'MAI POORI MAANAVTA KO SHRAAP DETA HU.'

articles.title: Anon Calls Buddhists 'Dumb', Sparks Debate
articles.body:
Anon questioned the intelligence of Buddhists, calling them 'straight up atheist' and claiming their religion promotes 'cuckery.' The poster praised Hindus, stating they are more open-minded and questioning.

Another anon countered that Buddhism was initially an elite phenomenon, with Buddha's earliest followers being brahmins and kshatriyas.

This anon also noted that Ambedkarite Buddhism, a political ideology with Buddhist flavour, is not recognised by actual Buddhist scholars.

Anon also exchanged views on the historical influence of Buddhism, citing examples of powerful Buddhist dynasties like the Kushans and Mongols.

One poster argued that Buddhism operates like parasitism, existing because other faiths allow it, and should be treated as such.
